computer-science-and-engg.name: Computer Science & Engineering
computer-science-and-engg.tagline: A Centre of Excellence in IT education, research and innovation since 1983.
computer-science-and-engg.overview: The Department of Computer Science and Engineering was established in 1983. It offers an under-graduate programme (B.E.) in Computer Science and Engineering with an intake of 180 students, along with M.Sc. (Engineering by Research) and Ph.D. programmes. The department has highly qualified and well-experienced faculty committed to imparting quality technical education in IT and research. Its primary objectives include developing innovative skills, enriching academics through quality teaching-learning processes, building student confidence and inculcating entrepreneurial abilities — producing professionals with ethical values and societal concern.
computer-science-and-engg.vision: To be a Centre of Excellence in IT education, research and innovation, crafting competent professionals catering to the needs of society with moral and ethical values.
mission: To produce competent IT professionals committed to lifelong learning.
To promote interdisciplinary research for addressing societal needs.
To inculcate leadership qualities and entrepreneurial abilities with moral and ethical values.
To instill environmental consciousness through teaching-learning processes.

peos.text: Provide a strong educational foundation in mathematics, science, basic engineering and Computer Science.
Develop proficiency with an ability to solve a wide range of computational problems in industry, government or other work environments.
Inculcate professionalism by infusing leadership qualities, team spirit, effective communication skills, high ethical values and empathy for the needs of society.
Encourage students towards higher studies, research activities and entrepreneurial skills for lifelong learning.

psos.text: Inculcate analytical, logical, programming and managerial abilities.
Apply Software Engineering principles and practices to provide software solutions.
Design and develop computational systems for real-life applications.

highlights: Established in 1983 with a sanctioned B.E. intake of 180 students.
18 Ph.D.s awarded under departmental guidance; 20 research scholars currently registered.
10 VTU-recognized research supervisors across pattern recognition, image processing, IoT and network security.
Dedicated research labs in multimedia & vision computing, wireless & mobile computing and data mining.
Active student forum (FOCUS) and industry MoUs with Infosys, IBM, Philips and C-DAC.

labs.name: Multimedia, Vision Computing and Intelligent Systems Lab
labs.description: Develops algorithms and systems for processing and understanding multimedia data including images, video and audio.

labs.name: Wireless and Mobile Computing Lab
labs.description: Builds practical understanding of wireless communication and mobile computing, with hands-on design and analysis of mobile networks.

labs.name: Data Mining and Information Systems Lab
labs.description: Develops and refines techniques for extracting useful knowledge from large datasets for better decision-making and insights.

labs.name: Learning Resources Lab
labs.description: Supports student learning through hands-on activities, experimentation and the application of knowledge.

labs.name: Named computing labs — Dijkstra, Charles Babbage, Kernighan & Ritchie, Kalpana Chawla, Alan Turing & C. V. Raman
labs.description: Modern computing labs equipped with 12th–14th Gen Intel Core i5/i7 HP desktops, multimedia projectors and air-conditioning.

activities.title: Workshop on Design Thinking, Critical Thinking and Innovative Thinking (IIC)
TechIgnite-2 — technical event series (Tech-Trivia, Capture the Flag, Algo Auction, Decode Escape)
Talk on Cyber Security by Mr. Nikhil Inganal, Threat Hunters LLP
Online Alumni Meet — 141 registered, 99 attended from across the world (incl. USA & UK)

associations.name: FOCUS — Forum of Computer Science Students
associations.about: A student association of UG and PG students that aids overall personality development. Run by the students and monitored by faculty, it organizes curricular, extra-curricular and socio-cultural activities and technical events exposing students to state-of-the-art tools and technologies.
